topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud, instantly sucking the air out of the room. "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise celebration for Sarah has been cancelled." I reread the sentence three times, the words swimming before my eyes. My hands, resting on the cool wooden desk, clenched into tight fists. A slow, cold burn started in my chest, spreading outwards. I didn't move for a full minute, just staring at the screen, the cheerful planning documents mocking me with their bright colors. When I finally pushed back from the desk, the sound of my chair scraping against the floor was unnecessarily loud. I walked to the window, the city lights blurring into streaks of meaningless color, feeling a profound emptiness settle deep within my core.

[story 2]

I stared at the confirmation text on my phone, the cheerful emojis from the original invitation now feeling like deliberate insults. Cancelled. The single word echoed in the sudden silence of my apartment. A sharp, unpleasant tightness constricted my throat, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I picked up the discarded party decorations—a streamer, a deflated balloon—and crumpled them slowly in my hand until the paper was a tight, angry ball. My jaw locked so hard I could feel the muscles trembling beneath my skin. Every muscle in my body felt coiled, ready to snap, but I kept my expression carefully neutral, a mask of practiced indifference sliding over my features.

[story 3]

When Mark finally delivered the news in person, his voice was light and breezy, completely at odds with the sinking feeling in my stomach. I watched him, noting the easy smile he wore, and felt a strange detachment. Internally, a deep, heavy weight seemed to settle just behind my sternum, making it hard to lean forward or relax. I managed a small, clipped response, something about needing to check on work, but the words felt hollow, brittle. I picked up my coffee mug, the ceramic warm against my palms, and instead of drinking, I just held it, the heat seeming to amplify the cold resolve hardening within me. I turned away from him without another word, the click of the door closing behind me sounding final and absolute.
